ddls:
CREATE TABLE "carbon_dioxide_measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the measurement */,
  "yy" INTEGER /* Year of the measurement */,
  "mm" INTEGER /* Month of the measurement */,
  "co2" REAL /* Measured CO2 concentration (ppm) */,
  "decimalyear" REAL /* Decimal representation of the year */,
  "polypart" REAL /* Polynomial part of the measurement analysis */,
  "harmpart" REAL /* Harmonic part of the measurement analysis */,
  "resid" REAL /* Residual of the measurement */,
  "resid_smooth" REAL /* Smoothed residual of the measurement */,
  "resid_trend" REAL /* Long-term trend in the residual data */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Calculated growth rate of CO2 concentration */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the measurement sites table */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the measurement types table */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the data sources table */,
  "quality_check_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the quality checks table */,
  "uncertainty" REAL /* Uncertainty associated with the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_carbon_dioxide_measurements_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_carbon_dioxide_measurements_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES measurement_sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_carbon_dioxide_measurements_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the site */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the site */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the site */,
  "instrument_type" TEXT /* Type of instrument used for measurements at the site */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement instrument was installed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement type (e.g., in-situ sampling, remote sensing) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement type */,
  "instrumentation" TEXT /* Specific instrumentation used for the measurement type */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the measurement type */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision of the measurement type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_checks" (
  "check_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement being checked */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date when the quality check was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the check (e.g., Pass, Fail) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments regarding the check */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the quality check */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Quality check protocol used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("check_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_checks_performed_by FOREIGN KEY ("performed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution or organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of data access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., view, download) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES carbon_dioxide_measurements ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend record */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement related to the trend */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the trend (e.g., increasing CO2 levels) */,
  "calculated_date" TEXT /* Date when the trend was calculated */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the trend */,
  "statistical_method" TEXT /* Statistical method used to derive the trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trends_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES carbon_dioxide_measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., satellite, ground station) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., remote sensing, in-situ) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "provider" TEXT /* Provider of the data source */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the data source is updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruments" (
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instrument */,
  "instrument_name" TEXT /* Name of the instrument */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of instrument */,
  "calibration_details" TEXT /* Calibration details of the instrument */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instrument_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "calibrations" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration */,
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instrument being calibrated */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the calibration was performed */,
  "results" TEXT /* Results of the calibration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id")
)
